defects.description: Multi-variable atomicity violation (DFC - Disjoint Field Correlation): Main task reads CmdTime.cmdtime[1] and cmdtime[2] to compute StarTime, but ISR can preempt and update both fields between the two reads, causing inconsistent calibration data to be used.
defects.root_cause: Missing critical section protection when reading correlated timestamp fields across multiple statements
defects.fix_suggestion: Use DisableExt6IRQ/EnableExt6IRQ to protect the multi-field read operation in CMU_cmd_respond
defects.impact: Star time calibration may use inconsistent timestamp values, leading to synchronization errors in the star tracker subsystem

defects.description: Multi-variable atomicity violation (TUP - Type Unsafe Pattern): Bus1553Init initializes Command_OBDH.Raw and Command_OBDH.Bit.Address sequentially. If SYS_ISRExt6 ISR triggers between the two writes, it reads partially initialized values, causing incorrect command parsing.
defects.root_cause: ISR enabled before complete initialization of shared union structure
defects.fix_suggestion: Disable interrupts before initializing Command_OBDH, re-enable after both fields are written
defects.impact: ISR may read inconsistent union state leading to incorrect 1553B command address parsing

defects.description: Multi-variable atomicity violation (TUP - Type Unsafe Pattern): Bus1553Init writes Command_OBDH fields while SYS_ISRExt6 ISR can concurrently write the same fields, resulting in race condition and potential data corruption.
defects.root_cause: Concurrent write access to shared union without synchronization
defects.fix_suggestion: Use critical section protection around initialization writes to Command_OBDH
defects.impact: Final state of Command_OBDH may be indeterminate due to interleaved writes from main and ISR contexts
